2603.26539.title: How Open Must Language Models be to Enable Reliable Scientific Inference?
2603.26539.paper_summary: How does the extent to which a model is open or closed impact the scientific inferences that can be drawn from research that involves it? In this paper, we analyze how restrictions on information about model construction and deployment threaten reliable inference. We argue that current closed models are generally ill-suited for scientific purposes, with some notable exceptions, and discuss ways in which the issues they present to reliable inference can be resolved or mitigated. We recommend that when models are used in research, potential threats to inference should be systematically identified along with the steps taken to mitigate them, and that specific justifications for model selection should be provided.
2603.26539.paper_summary_zh: 模型的開放或封閉程度如何影響從涉及該模型的研究中得出的科學推論？<lb/>在本文中，我們分析了有關模型構建和部署的信息限制如何威脅到可靠的推論。<lb/>我們認為，目前的封閉模型通常不適合科學目的，儘管有一些顯著的例外，並討論了如何解決或減輕它們對可靠推論所帶來的問題。<lb/>我們建議，在研究中使用模型時，應系統性地識別對推論的潛在威脅及其減輕措施，並提供模型選擇的具體理由。
2603.26539.author: James A. Michaelov et.al.
2603.26539.authors: James A. Michaelov, Catherine Arnett, Tyler A. Chang, Pamela D. Rivière, Samuel M. Taylor, Cameron R. Jones, Sean Trott, Roger P. Levy, Benjamin K. Bergen, Micah Altman
